changed.from: For m=1, evaluate the full-fiber compatibility definition at K=4 with source indices j=0,1,2 and cumulant orders r=2,3,4. For m=2, evaluate it at K=6 with j=0,1,2,3 and r=2,3,4,5,6.
changed.to: For m=1 and K=4, A_1 is the 12-equation incidence system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho^a+c_{2r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma^(r-a)+d_{2r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,3,4 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho=sigma=0, delta gamma=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{2r}, d_{2r}=c_{0r}gamma^r. For m=2 and K=6, A_2 is the 25-equation system t_{r,a}=c_{0r}gamma^a+c_{1r}rho_1^a+c_{2r}rho_2^a+c_{3r}1{a=r}=d_{0r}1{a=0}+d_{1r}sigma_1^(r-a)+d_{2r}sigma_2^(r-a)+d_{3r}delta^(r-a), for r=2,...,6 and 0<=a<=r, with the generic-locus disjunction; its common-axis subfamily is rho_1=sigma_1=0, delta gamma=1, sigma_2 rho_2=1, d_{0r}=c_{1r}, d_{1r}=c_{3r}, d_{2r}=c_{2r}rho_2^r, d_{3r}=c_{0r}gamma^r.
changed.reason: The open-ended handle only instructed the solver to evaluate the two systems. The proof derives these explicit coordinate equations and twin subfamilies, so the handle should record the resulting constructions rather than an unevaluated instruction.

changed.from: Use subresultants of the common-direction annihilating polynomial from the full-fiber compatibility equations together with real-root and truncated-moment inequalities for (c_{jr},d_{jr}) to stratify barE_m(R) and enumerate local real branches of both fiber correspondences.
changed.to: Put d=m+1, K=2d, and n=d+1. Convert each source cumulant list k=(k_2,...,k_K) to raw moments mu_r=B_r(0,k_2,...,k_r), and impose the finite atomic certificate Q_K(k): there exist w_h>=0,z_h in R (1<=h<=n) with sum_h w_h=1, sum_h w_h z_h^r=mu_r for 1<=r<=K, and mu_2>0. Compute defining equations J_m for barE_m by saturating the simultaneous equations Phi^right(theta)=t=Phi^left(eta) separately by the right and left genericity products, eliminating the parameter variables, and intersecting the two resulting t-ideals. Form Gamma_b={(t,lambda):J_m(t)=0, Phi^b(lambda)=t, the b-loading inequalities hold, and Q_K holds for every source list}. Run a simultaneous sign-invariant cylindrical algebraic decomposition, using coefficients, discriminants, and principal subresultants, with t ordered before lambda and atomic witnesses. The induced t-cells stratify barE_m(R); label each cell by whether its right and left Gamma_b stacks are nonempty, and output every selected section/sector stack as the local description of R^b_{m,K}(t) intersect F^b_{m,K}.
changed.reason: The original handle names useful ingredients but does not specify an exact finite moment-feasibility certificate, the equations of the complex exceptional closure, or the projection/lifting algorithm that decides both full real fibers. The corrected construction supplies all variables, equations, inequalities, elimination steps, and fiber outputs, and it treats singular and boundary moment sequences exactly.

note: maximality: minimize apolar sufficiency order L*(m)=m+1+s*(m)~m+sqrt(2m)
directive: MAXIMALITY IMPROVEMENT (strict strengthening by your own apolar argument; do NOT weaken any assumption or definition). In the sufficiency proofs of oeq:generic-separation (order 2m+2) and oeq:minimal-real-information-order (order 2m+1, m>=3), generic arrow identifiability is obtained by recovering the unordered loading-direction set D as the unique factor of the annihilator Q_D, which holds as soon as the degree-n apolar contraction stack B_D(e)=(sum_j c_{j,n+k} e_j ell_j^k)_{k=0}^{s-1}: C^n -> direct_sum_{k=0}^{s-1} Sym^k(C^2) attains rank n, i.e. as soon as its target dimension T_s = sum_{k=0}^{s-1}(k+1) = s(s+1)/2 >= n = m+2, using truncation order L = n+s-1. You only evaluated s=n-1 and s=n-2. DETERMINE THE MINIMAL SUFFICIENCY ORDER by minimizing L=n+s-1 over integers s>=1 subject to the rank threshold s(s+1)/2 >= m+2. Define s*(m)=min{s>=1: s(s+1)/2 >= m+2} and L*_suff(m)=(m+1)+s*(m); give s*(m) in closed form (s*(m)=ceil((sqrt(8m+17)-1)/2)) so L*_suff(m)=m+1+ceil((sqrt(8m+17)-1)/2) ~ m + sqrt(2m). PROVE generic arrow identifiability at truncation order L*_suff(m): (i) the partition/Vandermonde independence argument placing the n source indices into subsets J_k with |J_k|<=k+1 for k=0..s*-1 (total capacity T_{s*} >= n) makes B_D full-rank n at a generic parameter, so the contraction kernel is exactly <Q_D> and factoring Q_D returns all n loading directions; (ii) the forward direction set contains the vertical fixed axis and, off a proper real algebraic set, not the horizontal, while every reverse set contains the horizontal axis, so the arrow is distinguished; (iii) the real-feasibility open-set extension (positive compactly-supported density perturbation, triangular moment-to-cumulant map, product independence) survives verbatim since it is order-independent. Verify small cases against your existing results: m=1->L*=4=2m+2, m=2->L*=6=2m+2, m=3->L*=7=2m+1, m=4->L*=8=2m, m=5->L*=10=2m, m=10->L*=16, m=20->L*=28. UPGRADE THE HEADLINE: the arrow is generically identifiable from the truncated cumulant tensor at order L*_suff(m)=m+1+s*(m) ~ m+sqrt(2m), improving Chen et al.'s 2m+3 by (2m+3)-L*_suff(m) -> infinity orders (not one). Then RECONSIDER the exact minimal information order K*(m): is L*_suff(m) tight (i.e. does arrow ID FAIL at order L*_suff(m)-1, witnessed by a Euclidean-open real opposite-arrow twin family at that order, which requires T_{s*-1}=(s*-1)s*/2 < n so the annihilator is under-determined), or does the gap remain a residual open question? State K*(m) as a construct-and-determine result with the proven upper bound L*_suff(m) and the honest status of the matching lower bound. Keep the codimension-1 exceptional-locus theorem, the explicit m=1/m=2 incidence systems, and the real semialgebraic atlas unchanged; only sharpen the sufficiency ORDER and the K* framing.

note: D0.5 rubric: reposition both OEQs around resolved results (Q_D arrow functional; K*<=2m+1/L* improvement, exact min open)
directive: D0.5 RUBRIC novelty-positioning fixes — reposition BOTH open-ended questions around the RESOLVED proved results, not the original counterfactual questions (this composes with the round-2 maximality directive; apply both). (1) oeq:generic-separation: in its gap/consumer/positioning text, LEAD with the resolved contribution = the surviving simultaneous-binary-form (apolar Q_D) full-fiber ARROW functional that generically orients the pair from the truncated cumulant tensor on the separated domain; state plainly that the single-G_m-orbit / full same-arrow source-fiber claim is REFUTED (the fiber has m+1 label components and, for m>=2, dimension m(m-1)/2, and the ambient image codimension is m(m+1)/2 not m), and that the arrow functional is what SURVIVES and is the contribution. Compare it PRECISELY to ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction Theorems 4.4-4.7: their k=m+2 cumulant-matrix rank test reaches order 2m+3 and only orients, whereas this is a full-fiber apolar direction recovery at strictly lower truncation order together with an explicit exceptional non-identification locus. (2) oeq:minimal-real-information-order: rewrite its gap/consumer so they describe the PROVED strict order improvement rather than a positive minimality resolution: by the round-2 apolar-order minimization, generic arrow identifiability holds at truncation order L*(m)=m+1+s*(m) with s*(m)=min{s: s(s+1)/2>=m+2} (~ m+sqrt(2m)), in particular at order 2m+1 for m>=3, strictly below Chen's 2m+3 by an unbounded margin as m grows. Name the closest-literature comparison and state CLEARLY that the EXACT minimal information order K*(m) remains OPEN: the proved upper bound is L*(m), and the matching lower bound (a Euclidean-open real opposite-arrow twin family at order L*(m)-1) is the residual open question. Do not reintroduce any refuted positive claim; position strictly on what is proved.

note: WHOLESALE: sharpen thm order to L*(m); full comparator set incl ICLR-2026 2m+2; discharge atlas real-QE/CAD substrate
directive:
WHOLESALE D0.5 ROOT FIX (address all remaining classes at once; do NOT patch one finding per round; do NOT weaken any assumption). THREE coordinated changes:

(A) SHARPEN THE ORDER of thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery from K=2m+2 to the MINIMAL apolar sufficiency order, and restate it there. The theorem's own proof recovers the unordered loading-direction set D as the unique factor of the support annihilator Q_D as soon as the degree-n apolar contraction stack B_D(e)=(sum_j c_{j,n+k} e_j ell_j^k)_{k=0}^{s-1} attains rank n, i.e. as soon as its target dimension T_s=s(s+1)/2 >= n=m+2, using truncation order L=n+s-1. Minimize L over s: define s*(m)=min{s>=1: s(s+1)/2 >= m+2}=ceil((sqrt(8m+17)-1)/2) and L*(m)=(m+1)+s*(m) ~ m+sqrt(2m). RESTATE thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery at truncation order L*(m) (verify: m=1->4, m=2->6, m=3->7, m=4->8, m=5->10, m=10->16, m=20->28), keeping the same three proof steps (partition/Vandermonde full-rank at the minimal s; vertical-vs-horizontal fixed-axis arrow decision against every opposite-arrow fiber; order-independent real-feasibility open-set extension). This is a STRICT strengthening by the theorem's own argument.

(B) COMPLETE COMPARATOR SET for thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery (write the WHOLE differentiation table, not one comparator): systematically differentiate against EVERY close approach on the axes (model / assumptions / order / deliverable=orient-only-vs-full-fiber-recovery+exceptional-locus): (i) ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction (k=m+2 cumulant-matrix rank test, reaches order 2m+3, orient-only); (ii) the contemporaneous ICLR-2026 rank characterization using cumulants through order 2m+2 that the referee flagged — state its model, assumptions, and that it is a RANK/orientation criterion at order 2m+2, then differentiate: this work gives a full-fiber APOLAR RECOVERY MAP (factor Q_D) plus an explicit codimension-1 exceptional non-identification locus and its real semialgebraic atlas, at the strictly lower order L*(m) < 2m+2 for all m>=3, so it is neither subsumed by nor duplicative of an order-2m+2 rank test; (iii) SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 (multivariate higher-cumulant rank/fiber, not the bivariate full-fiber overlap); (iv) TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 (finite-root recovery for a specified graph + proxy/instrument, not the order frontier); (v) CaiGaoHara2025Direction / CaiHara2026 / ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 (dependent-latent or one-latent or sparsest-DAG, not the arbitrary-m independent-source bivariate order frontier). If the referee's ICLR-2026 comparator cannot be verified to exist, differentiate on the robust invariant axes anyway (full-fiber recovery + exceptional-locus deliverable at order L*(m), versus any rank/orientation test), which does not depend on that paper existing.

(C) DISCHARGE THE STANDARD SUBSTRATE for oeq:real-exceptional-atlas: the finite semialgebraic stratification / cell decision procedure rests on STANDARD real quantifier elimination and sign-invariant cylindrical algebraic decomposition — cite these as standard primitives (Tarski-Seidenberg quantifier elimination; Basu-Pollack-Roy, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, for CAD and sign-invariant stratification) so the atlas construction is grounded in cited standard results rather than an undischarged substrate. Add these references to the bibliography.

Also update oeq:generic-separation, oeq:minimal-real-information-order, and the estimand/direction-selector text to be CONSISTENT with the sharper order L*(m): the proved sufficiency upper bound on the minimal information order K*(m) is L*(m) (in particular <=2m+1 for m>=3, and < 2m+2 for all m>=3), with the matching lower bound (real opposite-arrow twins at order L*(m)-1) remaining the honest residual open question. Keep the codimension-1 exceptional-locus theorem and the real atlas. Do not reintroduce any refuted positive claim (single-G_m-orbit, codim-m, all-m equality K*=2m+2).
